title: Improve documentation of (podman image save)
body:
#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Improve documentation of `podman image save`, partially motivated by https://github.com/containers/common/pull/820 .

#### How to verify it

N/A

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mtrmac* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mtrmac] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM
Better, but I still question why we want all of these formats, when we have transports.

I mean, we’re not designing this from scratch; the features exist in Podman now and I don’t think we can just trivially drop them like that.

Specific things that `podman image save` does that raw use of transports doesn’t:
- It works (I think?) with remote connections, transferring the data from the to the client. Just using a c/image transport would result in files written to the server (which is why IIRC attempts to do so are summarily refused).
- It automatically uses the name of the source image in the produced data, unlike `podman push $source $transport:…`, where the destination is purely user-specified and exactly as provided by the user.  E.g. `podman image save busybox -o ./dest.tar` would have to be entered as something like `podman push busybox docker-archive:./dest.tar:localhost/busybox`.

> Better, but I still question why we want all of these formats, when we have transports.

Somebody in the past mixed _formats_ and _transports_ which we agree on is confusing but it exists like that for a very long time.
